channel.title: Rust on Home
channel.description: Recent content in Rust on Home

item.title: Notes on reading halloy source code
item.description:
Intro

Hey, how’s it going ? Hope you’re doing well.

From some days, i have been trying out iced direct from create and soruce code but whenever i write it i feel i am not writing rust in a idomatic way. So i thought to read a open source codebase to understand a popular project on how to write a system from start to end, find design decisions, why something is written in someway and how can i learn from it and write my own applications end to end.

item.title: Building a Persistent KV Store with B-epsilon Trees
item.description:
Research Notes

I want to build a persistent key-value store based on B-epsilon trees. I looked at LevelDB but it’s written in C++, which isn’t my preference. So I decided to write this in Rust since I haven’t touched it in a while. I’m also a bit tired of Go at this point due to its syntax, error handling, and GC overhead.

Understanding LSM Trees

I started by learning about LSM trees. ScyllaDB has a good introductory article on it:
